channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom - CBS News
item.description:
1. Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom  CBS News
  2. Saturday’s meteor was 5 feet wide and exceptionally rare. Here’s more information about the remarkable event.  The Boston Globe
  3. Meteor causes explosion reports, sightings from Delaware to Montreal  WCAX
  4. Footage shows meteorite entering atmosphere  WJAR
  5. Meteor Explosion Caught on Camera in Rome  WKTV

item.title: May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026 - Space
item.description:
1. May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026  Space
  2. Clear skies give Metro Detroit perfect Blue Moon viewing weather  ClickOnDetroit | WDIV Local 4

  4. How to Best See This Weekend’s Rare Blue Micromoon  Time Magazine
  5. What is a blue micromoon and when is the best time to see it this weekend?  The Guardian

item.description:

item.title: Scientists Find Cosmic 'Rosetta Stone' To Decode Baffling Signals From Deep Space - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Find Cosmic 'Rosetta Stone' To Decode Baffling Signals From Deep Space  ScienceAlert
  2. Mysterious signals keep coming from space. We have found their ‘Rosetta stone’  The Conversation
  3. Periodic radio and X-ray emission from an accreting white dwarf binary  Nature
  4. Astronomers might have decoded a mysterious signal from space  The Independent
  5. Scientists Say They’ve Cracked Mystery Behind a Dozen Strange Signals From Deep Space  Futurism

item.title: Scientists reveal the origin of the Euphrates — a river that fed the 'cradle of civilization' - Live Science
item.description:
1. Scientists reveal the origin of the Euphrates — a river that fed the 'cradle of civilization'  Live Science
  2. Late Miocene Euphrates River drained into a partially desiccated eastern Mediterranean  Nature
  3. Scientists may have just solved the Euphrates River’s mysterious origins  National Geographic
  4. The Origin of the Euphrates Discovered: The Great River of Mesopotamia Was Born 6 Million Years Ago and Flowed Into an Almost Dry Mediterranean  La Brújula Verde

item.title: Mars was once a warmer world of rivers, lakes and a thicker atmosphere, but after its internal dynamo died and the planet lost the magnetic shield that helps protect an atmosphere, the solar wind stripped much of its air away over billions of years, leaving the c - Space Daily

item.title: Supermassive black holes could be the universe's biggest planet nurseries - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Supermassive black holes could be the universe's biggest planet nurseries  Phys.org
  2. Supermassive black holes could be prolific planet-making machines  New Atlas
  3. Supermassive black holes give rise to billions of planets  Universe Space Tech
  4. Millions of planets might form around supermassive black holes  New Scientist

item.title: When Rosetta sniffed the gas around Comet 67P, it found a cloud that would have smelled of rotten eggs, ammonia and bitter almonds — and hidden in that cosmic stink were some of the chemical ingredients that may have helped life begin on Earth - Space Daily
item.description: When Rosetta sniffed the gas around Comet 67P, it found a cloud that would have smelled of rotten eggs, ammonia and bitter almonds — and hidden in that cosmic stink were some of the chemical ingredients that may have helped life begin on Earth  Space Daily

item.title: CERN Finds High-Significance Hint of Physics Beyond Standard Model - Ground News
item.description:
1. CERN Finds High-Significance Hint of Physics Beyond Standard Model  Ground News
  2. These exotic particles could break physics  Scientific American
  3. Large Hadron Collider detects strange particle behavior that could rewrite physics  ScienceDaily
  4. Analysis of B-meson decay hints at new physics  Physics Today
  5. At the world's largest particle accelerator, CERN physicists observed a particle behaving in a way that theory did not predict, the strongest indication so far of physics beyond what is currently known.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.description:

  4. Amputated sea cucumber tissue keeps living for years—possibly forever  Scientific American
  5. Scientists find groundbreaking potential for tissue regrowth and immortality in the ocean  EurekAlert!

item.title: Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization' - Space
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization'  Space

  3. Astrophysicists strike black gold with treasure trove of gravitational wave detections  Phys.org
  4. The Universe Is Full of ‘Impossible’ Black Holes. Scientists Now Know Why  WIRED
  5. Gravitational waves confirm predicted black-hole mass gap and probe stellar nuclear physics  Nature

item.description: Mathematician solves origami donut efficiency challenge with fewest folds  Phys.org

item.title: Cats can't taste sweetness — evolution turned off the relevant gene in their distant ancestors when they became obligate carnivores, and without working sweet receptors, a cat is as indifferent to sugar as a person is to ultraviolet light - Space Daily
item.description: Cats can't taste sweetness — evolution turned off the relevant gene in their distant ancestors when they became obligate carnivores, and without working sweet receptors, a cat is as indifferent to sugar as a person is to ultraviolet light  Space Daily

  4. Bypassing the 14-day rule: China’s ‘artificial embryos’ exploit a double loophole in orbit  WION

item.title: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating - Space Daily
item.description: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating  Space Daily

item.title: About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour - Space Daily
item.description: About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour  Space Daily

item.title: There is a fish that can live more than a hundred years — the rougheye rockfish, which inhabits the deep waters of the North Pacific — and the slow rate at which it grows, breeds, and ages means that some of the individuals being caught by fishermen today w - Space Daily
item.description: There is a fish that can live more than a hundred years — the rougheye rockfish, which inhabits the deep waters of the North Pacific — and the slow rate at which it grows, breeds, and ages means that some of the individuals being caught by fishermen today w  Space Daily

item.title: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin - Space Daily
item.description: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin  Space Daily

item.title: There are about 20 quadrillion ants alive on Earth at any moment — enough that their combined biomass outweighs every wild bird and mammal on the planet combined, even though the often-repeated claim that ant biomass equals human biomass has been - Space Daily

item.title: Bolted to Pioneer 10 is a plaque showing two humans and a cosmic map back to Earth, while the spacecraft itself is now a silent ghost ship drifting toward Aldebaran, a star it will not pass for another two million years. - Space Daily
item.description: Bolted to Pioneer 10 is a plaque showing two humans and a cosmic map back to Earth, while the spacecraft itself is now a silent ghost ship drifting toward Aldebaran, a star it will not pass for another two million years.  Space Daily

item.title: When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu - Space Daily
item.description:
1. When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu  Space Daily
  2. Chinese startup Mega Engine advances reusable staged-combustion rocket engine  SpaceNews
  3. China successfully fires reusable engine to compete with SpaceX  Interesting Engineering
  4. Chinese Startup Fires ‘Blazing’ Oxygen-Rich Engine for Deep Space Missions  Gizmodo

item.title: One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does - Phys.org
item.description: One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does  Phys.org

item.title: After a crippled seven-year journey Japan's Hayabusa probe limped home in 2010 and burned up in the sky over Australia, but not before releasing the first asteroid samples ever returned to Earth. - Space Daily
item.description: After a crippled seven-year journey Japan's Hayabusa probe limped home in 2010 and burned up in the sky over Australia, but not before releasing the first asteroid samples ever returned to Earth.  Space Daily

item.title: The Moon is stealing time from the Earth, and it has been getting away with it for billions of years. Our planet spins so much slower than it once did that a single day has stretched from just 19 hours to the 24 we live by, and the Moon is still creeping away from u - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Moon is stealing time from the Earth, and it has been getting away with it for billions of years. Our planet spins so much slower than it once did that a single day has stretched from just 19 hours to the 24 we live by, and the Moon is still creeping away from u  Space Daily
  2. A Day on Earth Is Getting Longer as the Planet’s Rotation Slows  WSJ
  3. Are we running out of 24 hour days? Climate change is slightly stretching time  Business Today
  4. Scientists Say Climate Change Is Making Earth's Days Longer Than Before  NDTV
  5. A day on Earth is described as 24 hours, but the planet has been slowing down for billions of years — and growth rings in ancient coral fossils show that 380 million years ago, hundreds of millions of years before any dinosaur existed, an Earth day was only ab  Space Daily

item.title: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer  Space Daily

item.title: Human Brain Cells Grown on a Chip Level Up to Play 'Doom' - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Human Brain Cells Grown on a Chip Level Up to Play 'Doom'  ScienceAlert
  2. GPU is not the end game in AI, but biological neurons  Sebastian Barros Newsletter
  3. 🧠 Here is the first living 3d neural network for AI  Techno-Science.net
  4. Lab-Grown Brain Organoids Propel Advances in Biocomputer Technology  Bioengineer.org
  5. Brain cells learn gaming in surprise experiment  Minute Mirror

item.title: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion. - Space Daily
item.description: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion.  Space Daily

item.description:
1. Scientists discover 5.5 million bees living beneath a New York cemetery for over 100 years and it could h  The Times of India
  2. Millions of Bees Have Thrived Under a New York Cemetery for More Than a Century  WIRED
  3. A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground  ScienceDaily
  4. Scientists find 5.5 million bees hiding underground in New York | Bees may have lived there for over 100 years | Inshorts  Inshorts
  5. A New York Cemetery Was Hiding Over 5 Million Burrowing Bees, One of the World's Largest Concentrations  Good News Network

item.title: Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us  ScienceAlert
  2. Something Just Passed Between Us and a Distant Star.  Universe Today
  3. An Unknown Object Passed Between Earth And A Star In The Large Magellanic Cloud, In An Event That Lasted An Hour  IFLScience
  4. Astronomers may have detected a primordial black hole for the first time  UA.NEWS
  5. A glow lasting only 1 hour observed in space in 2019 may have revealed a primordial black hole with a mass of 3 moons, associated with dark matter and considered 100,000 times more likely than common stellar objects by scientists in the study.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

  4. Different meteorites, same birthplace  EurekAlert!

item.title: 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe  Live Science
  2. The Galaxy That Forgot to Spin  Universe Today
  3. The James Webb Telescope photographed a gigantic galaxy less than 2 billion years after the Big Bang that simply does not rotate, a behavior astronomers only expected to see in much older galaxies.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: Your Blood May Carry a 700-Million-Year-Old Secret - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. Your Blood May Carry a 700-Million-Year-Old Secret  SciTechDaily
  2. Scientists discover ancient single-celled ancestors still live on in your blood  ScienceDaily
  3. Your Blood May Contain an Evolutionary Relic Older Than Animals Themselves  ScienceAlert
  4. Study reveals the evolution of the most ancient human blood cell  Interesting Engineering
  5. The blood running through your veins has a 700-million-year-old story, and scientists just worked out how it begins  The Times of India
